 {"id":"bd-2nknt","title":"Feature: Time-Boxed Ensembles","description":"## Summary
Allow users to set a maximum runtime for ensemble runs with graceful degradation when time expires.

## Rationale
Time-boxing ensures predictable completion and still returns partial value when a full run would be too slow.

## Implementation
- Add `--timeout` (duration) to `ntm ensemble run`.
- Prioritize modes using metadata (tier/priority) and expected value vs. cost.
- Graceful degradation:
  - Complete in-flight modes
  - Skip remaining low-priority modes
  - Synthesize available outputs
- Report skipped modes + reason; suggest a better timeout.

## Logging
- Log timeout budget, mode ordering decisions, and skipped modes.

## Tests
- Unit: prioritization order + cutoff logic
- Integration: timeout triggers partial synthesis
- E2E: forced timeout produces partial results + resume hint

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Timeout flag accepted (e.g., 30m, 1h)
- [ ] Mode ordering respects priority metadata
- [ ] Partial synthesis completes correctly
- [ ] Skipped modes reported with reasons
- [ ] Logs include timeout + skip details

## Dependencies
- Requires EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- Requires Budget/Resource Control (bd-1dt84)
- Requires mode priority metadata (bd-3806s)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:06:34.840052020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:55:56.870698442Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nknt","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2nknt","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2nknt","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2nknt","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nnsg","title":"Feature: Real-time Synthesis Streaming","description":"## Summary
Stream synthesis output as it is generated (deterministic chunk ordering), with safe cancel/resume and no behavior changes when streaming is disabled.

## Rationale
Long synthesis steps feel opaque. Streaming:
- Reduces perceived latency
- Exposes progress and partial reasoning
- Enables early cancellation while preserving useful work

## Implementation
- Extend SynthesisEngine to emit ordered `SynthesisChunk` events (finding | conclusion | conflict | progress | status) with monotonic index and optional mode_id.
- Streaming is a view over the same synthesis logic (no divergence from non-stream behavior).
- CLI: `ntm ensemble synthesize --stream` prints incremental output; `--stream --json` emits JSONL events + final summary envelope.
- TUI/dashboard: incremental updates (no SSE/web unless an HTTP API already exists).
- Integrate with checkpointing/resume (bd-3didt) and state persistence (bd-25wul).

## Logging
- Log stream start/end, run_id, chunk_count, time_to_first_chunk, cancel reason.

## Tests
- Unit: chunk ordering + deterministic emission
- Integration: stream + synthesis fixture
- E2E: CLI stream output, Ctrl+C resume hint, robot JSONL schema

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Streaming begins before synthesis completes
- [ ] Chunk ordering is deterministic
- [ ] Cancel preserves partial results with resume command
- [ ] Non-stream path is unchanged
- [ ] Structured logs include run_id + stream stats","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:05:27.296514586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:53:49.537945953Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nnsg","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2nnsg","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2nnsg","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2nnsg","depends_on_id":"bd-3didt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2o8r","title":"Task: Implement webhook manager and HTTP dispatcher","description":"## Overview
Create the core webhook infrastructure for dispatching HTTP requests on events.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/webhook/manager.go:
   - WebhookManager struct
   - Register(config WebhookConfig) error
   - Dispatch(event Event) error
   - Start() - begin background processing
   - Stop() - graceful shutdown

2. HTTP dispatcher:
   - Async dispatch (don't block event source)
   - Configurable timeout per webhook
   - Connection pooling for efficiency
   - Request signing (optional HMAC)

3. Retry logic:
   - Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 5 retries
   - Configurable retry policy per webhook
   - Dead letter queue for failed webhooks
   - Retry on 5xx errors, not 4xx

4. Event queueing:
   - In-memory queue with bounded size
   - Drop oldest events if queue full (log warning)
   - Batch dispatch for high-volume events

## Implementation Notes
- Use worker goroutine pool for dispatching
- Context cancellation for graceful shutdown
- Structured logging for debugging failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- Webhooks dispatch asynchronously
- Retries work with exponential backoff
- Failed webhooks logged, don't crash NTM
- Unit tests for dispatch and retry logic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:23.033786619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:48:43.266930100Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:48:43.266885987Z","close_reason":"Implemented webhook manager and HTTP dispatcher with all features: async dispatch, connection pooling, exponential backoff retry (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s), dead letter queue, HMAC signing, bounded event queue, worker goroutine pool. 17 tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2o8r","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2oc4s","title":"Test: Integration test for full swarm lifecycle","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AutoRespawner kill and respawn sequences, validating agent lifecycle management.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/respawner_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "context"
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestAutoRespawner_KillSequence(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        name          string
        agentType     string
        paneID        string
        expectedKeys  []string // Expected key sequences sent
    }{
        {
            name:      "claude_code_kill",
            agentType: "cc",
            paneID:    "cc_agents_1:1.0",
            expectedKeys: []string{
                "C-c",        // Interrupt
                "C-c",        // Double interrupt
                "/exit",      // Claude Code exit command
                "Enter",
            },
        },
        {
            name:      "codex_kill",
            agentType: "cod",
            paneID:    "cod_agents_1:1.0",
            expectedKeys: []string{
                "C-c",
                "exit",
                "Enter",
            },
        },
        {
            name:      "gemini_kill",
            agentType: "gmi",
            paneID:    "gmi_agents_1:1.0",
            expectedKeys: []string{
                "C-c",
                "q",  // Quit key
            },
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            mockTmux.Reset()
            
            t.Logf("Testing kill sequence for %s agent", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            err := respawner.KillAgent(tt.paneID, tt.agentType)
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            sentKeys := mockTmux.GetSentKeys(tt.paneID)
            t.Logf("Keys sent: %v", sentKeys)
            t.Logf("Expected: %v", tt.expectedKeys)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectedKeys, sentKeys, "Kill sequence mismatch")
        })
    }
}

func TestAutoRespawner_ForceKill(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = mockTmux
    respawner.killTimeout = 100 * time.Millisecond
    
    // Simulate unresponsive agent
    mockTmux.SetProcessRunning("test:1.0", true)
    mockTmux.SetKillPaneResult("test:1.0", nil)
    
    t.Log("Testing force kill on unresponsive agent")
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    err := respawner.ForceKill(ctx, "test:1.0")
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    // Verify kill-pane was called
    assert.True(t, mockTmux.KillPaneCalled("test:1.0"), "kill-pane should be called")
    t.Log("Force kill successful - kill-pane was invoked")
}

func TestAutoRespawner_Respawn(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := NewMockTmuxClient()
    mockLauncher := NewMockPaneLauncher()
    
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = mockTmux
    respawner.launcher = mockLauncher
    respawner.respawnDelay = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    paneSpec := PaneSpec{
        Index:     1,
        Project:   "/home/user/project",
        AgentType: "cc",
    }
    
    t.Logf("Testing respawn for pane %d", paneSpec.Index)
    t.Logf("Project: %s", paneSpec.Project)
    t.Logf("Agent type: %s", paneSpec.AgentType)
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    startTime := time.Now()
    
    err := respawner.Respawn(ctx, "cc_agents_1", paneSpec)
    
    elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
    t.Logf("Respawn completed in %v", elapsed)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    // Verify respawn delay was respected
    assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, elapsed, respawner.respawnDelay, 
        "Should respect respawn delay")
    
    // Verify launcher was called
    assert.True(t, mockLauncher.LaunchCalled(), "Launcher should be invoked")
    t.Logf("Launch args: session=%s, pane=%v", 
        mockLauncher.LastSession(), mockLauncher.LastPaneSpec())
}

func TestAutoRespawner_RespawnWithCallback(t *testing.T) {
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner.launcher = NewMockPaneLauncher()
    
    callbackCalled := false
    var receivedEvent RespawnCompleteEvent
    
    respawner.OnComplete(func(event RespawnCompleteEvent) error {
        callbackCalled = true
        receivedEvent = event
        t.Logf("Callback received: %+v", event)
        return nil
    })
    
    paneSpec := PaneSpec{
        Index:     2,
        Project:   "/test/project",
        AgentType: "cod",
    }
    
    t.Log("Testing respawn with completion callback")
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    err := respawner.Respawn(ctx, "cod_agents_1", paneSpec)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    assert.True(t, callbackCalled, "Callback should be invoked")
    assert.Equal(t, "cod", receivedEvent.AgentType)
    assert.Equal(t, "/test/project", receivedEvent.Project)
    
    t.Log("Callback verification complete")
}

func TestAutoRespawner_ConcurrentRespawns(t *testing.T) {
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner.launcher = NewMockPaneLauncher()
    respawner.respawnDelay = 10 * time.Millisecond
    
    panes := []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc", Project: "/p1"},
        {Index: 2, AgentType: "cc", Project: "/p2"},
        {Index: 3, AgentType: "cc", Project: "/p3"},
    }
    
    t.Logf("Testing concurrent respawn of %d panes", len(panes))
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    var wg sync.WaitGroup
    errors := make(chan error, len(panes))
    
    startTime := time.Now()
    
    for _, pane := range panes {
        wg.Add(1)
        go func(p PaneSpec) {
            defer wg.Done()
            if err := respawner.Respawn(ctx, "cc_agents_1", p); err != nil {
                errors <- err
            }
        }(pane)
    }
    
    wg.Wait()
    close(errors)
    
    elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
    t.Logf("All respawns completed in %v", elapsed)
    
    for err := range errors {
        t.Errorf("Respawn error: %v", err)
    }
```

 {"id":"bd-35bn4","title":"Feature: Auto-Ensemble Selection - Recommend best preset from question analysis","description":"# Feature: Auto-Ensemble Selection

## Problem Statement
Users face choice paralysis with 9 ensemble presets. They ask:
- Which ensemble is best for my question?
- What's the difference between bug-hunt and root-cause?
- Am I picking the right one?

## Solution
Analyze question text and recommend the best-fit ensemble with explanation (deterministic, rule-based).

## User Experience
```bash
ntm ensemble suggest "What security vulnerabilities exist in the auth system?"
# Output:
# Recommended: safety-risk
# Confidence: HIGH
#
# Reason: matched keywords: security, vulnerabilities → safety-risk
#
# Alternatives:
#   bug-hunt (0.65) - If looking for specific implementation bugs
#   root-cause (0.45) - If investigating known security incident
#
# To run: ntm ensemble spawn safety-risk --question "..."
```

## Piping Support
```bash
# Get just the ID for piping
ntm ensemble suggest --question "What security..." --id-only
# Output: safety-risk

# Pipe to spawn
ntm ensemble spawn $(ntm ensemble suggest --question "..." --id-only) --question "..."
```

## Matching Algorithm (Deterministic)
Pattern-based (no ML) for reliability:

```go
var ensembleKeywords = map[string][]string{
    "project-diagnosis":   {"architecture", "structure", "codebase", "overview", "health"},
    "idea-forge":          {"feature", "improve", "add", "enhance", "brainstorm", "ideas"},
    "spec-critique":       {"spec", "requirements", "api", "interface", "contract"},
    "safety-risk":         {"security", "vulnerability", "threat", "compliance", "risk"},
    "architecture-review": {"design", "pattern", "refactor", "structure", "scalable"},
    "tech-debt-triage":    {"debt", "legacy", "cleanup", "deprecat", "upgrade"},
    "bug-hunt":            {"bug", "error", "fail", "broken", "issue"},
    "root-cause":          {"why", "cause", "investigate", "debug", "trace"},
    "strategic-planning":  {"roadmap", "priority", "plan", "strategy", "quarter"},
}
```

Scoring rules:
- Lowercase + simple stemming (suffix trim)
- Keyword count × weights
- Tie-breaker: preset priority order
- Confidence = normalized score → HIGH/MED/LOW

## Robot API
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-suggest --question="..."
# Returns: {ensemble_id, confidence, reason, alternatives: [...]}
```

## Logging
- Log matched keywords + scores
- Log chosen preset + confidence

## Tests
- Unit: scoring + tie-breakers
- Unit: id-only output
- E2E: suggest + spawn piping
- E2E: robot JSON schema + deterministic ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] suggest command analyzes question text
- [ ] Returns top match with confidence score
- [ ] Shows 2-3 alternatives with scores
- [ ] Explains why each was matched/ranked
- [ ] --id-only flag for piping

```go
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{

        {"tier3_maximum", 99, 3},

    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Testing bead count %d, expecting tier %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier)
            got := calc.CalculateTier(tt.beadCount)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTier, got, 
                "beadCount=%d should be tier %d, got %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier, got)
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateProjectAllocation(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
        Tier1Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 3, Cod: 2, Gmi: 1},

            wantCC:    2, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 4,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier3_project",

func TestCalculateAllocations_Sorting(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := defaultTestConfig()
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
```

 {"id":"bd-384s3","title":"caut: Implement CAAM integration for coordinated alerts","description":"Coordinate caut alerts with CAAM for automatic account switching.

## Background

Both caut and CAAM track usage/limits. Need to avoid duplication and coordinate:
- caut: historical tracking, cost visibility, projections
- CAAM: real-time rate limit response, account switching

## Integration Points

### 1. Unified Alert Source
Use caut as the primary source for proactive alerts.
CAAM handles reactive (actual rate limit hit).

### 2. Pre-emptive Switching
When caut reports >90% usage for a provider:
1. Alert user via dashboard
2. Optionally trigger CAAM switch before hitting actual limit
3. Avoid the rate limit entirely

### 3. Data Sharing
Consider feeding caut data to CAAM for:
- Better account selection (pick account with most headroom)
- Cooldown predictions
- Cost optimization across accounts

## Implementation

```go
func (m *IntegrationManager) OnCautAlert(provider string, usagePercent int) {
    if usagePercent >= 90 && m.config.CAAM.Enabled && m.config.CAAM.AutoRotate {
        // Proactive switch before hitting limit
        m.caam.SwitchAccount(provider)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-38jwx","title":"AutoRespawner: Force kill via process PID when needed","description":"## AutoRespawner: Force Kill via Process PID

### Purpose
Implement force kill fallback when graceful termination fails.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/auto_respawner.go`

**Force Kill:**
```go
// forceKill sends SIGKILL to the process in the pane.
func (r *AutoRespawner) forceKill(sessionPane string) error {
    log.Warn("[AutoRespawner] force_kill_start", "session_pane", sessionPane)
    
    // Get the PID of the foreground process in the pane
    // tmux display-message -p -t <pane> '#{pane_pid}'
    pid, err := r.getPanePID(sessionPane)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("get pane pid: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Kill the process tree
    // Use kill -9 on the process group for thorough cleanup
    cmd := exec.Command("kill", "-9", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", pid))
    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
        // Try just the process if group kill fails
        cmd = exec.Command("kill", "-9", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid))
        if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("kill -9 failed: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    log.Info("[AutoRespawner] force_kill_complete", "session_pane", sessionPane, "pid", pid)
    return nil
}

// getPanePID gets the foreground process PID from tmux.
func (r *AutoRespawner) getPanePID(sessionPane string) (int, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "display-message", "-p", "-t", sessionPane, "#{pane_pid}")
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return 0, fmt.Errorf("display-message: %w", err)
    }
    
    pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(output)))
    if err != nil {
        return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse pid %q: %w", output, err)
    }
    
    return pid, nil
}
```

**Full Kill Sequence:**
```go
// killWithFallback tries graceful kill, then force kill.
func (r *AutoRespawner) killWithFallback(ctx context.Context, sessionPane, agentType string) error {
    // Try graceful kill first
    if err := r.killAgent(ctx, sessionPane, agentType); err != nil {
        log.Warn("[AutoRespawner] graceful_kill_failed", "error", err)
    }
    
    // Wait for exit
    if r.waitForExit(ctx, sessionPane, r.GracefulTimeout) {
        return nil
    }
    
    // Fall back to force kill
    if err := r.forceKill(sessionPane); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("force kill failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Wait again briefly
    time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
    return nil
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log force kill start: `[AutoRespawner] force_kill_start session_pane=%s`
- Log PID lookup: `[AutoRespawner] pid_lookup session_pane=%s pid=%d`
- Log force kill complete: `[AutoRespawner] force_kill_complete session_pane=%s pid=%d`
- Log force kill failure: `[AutoRespawner] force_kill_failed session_pane=%s error=%v`

- [ ] Get PID via tmux display-message
- [ ] Kill process group with SIGKILL
- [ ] Fallback to individual process kill
- [ ] Combine with graceful kill in sequence","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:22.852466021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:26:35.728016771Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:26:35.727966607Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38jwx","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38jwx","depends_on_id":"bd-8uiw0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## What It Does
When NTM spawns agents—whether after a crash, reboot, manual restart, or returning after hours/days—it automatically reconstructs the context each agent needs to continue exactly where it left off.

## Why This Is The Best Idea
- The #1 frustration in multi-agent workflows is context loss
- Every session restart currently feels like starting over
- This transforms restarts from painful to seamless
- Users will feel like their agents have 'memory'

## How It Works
On session spawn/respawn:
1. Check for prior state: Look for existing checkpoints, Agent Mail threads, and session history
2. Query Agent Mail: Fetch the last N messages from threads this session participated in
3. Query CM (CASS Memory): Retrieve relevant procedural memories for this project
4. Query BV: Get current in-progress beads assigned to this session
5. Synthesize recovery prompt: Construct a structured 'here's what you were doing' context injection
6. Auto-send on spawn: Each agent receives this context automatically before user's first prompt

## Flywheel Alignment: Perfect
- Integrates checkpoints (already built)
- Integrates Agent Mail (already built)
- Integrates CM (part of flywheel)
- Integrates BV (part of flywheel)
- Each use makes recovery context richer

## Technical Implementation
- Changes to spawn.go: buildRecoveryContext() function
- New config options under [recovery] section
- Graceful degradation: works with any subset of integrations available
